title: narcissus longispathus subsp. yepesii
intro: Narcissus longispathus subsp. yepesii, also known as the Spanish bluebell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its bell-shaped blue flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Narcissus longispathus subsp. yepesii are believed to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as potential antiseptic and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
